title: Cross-Fitted Specialist Blend With Calibration
summary: ROC AUC is rank-based, so calibrating a single model does not help much directly, but blending multiple specialists can fail if their score scales are misaligned.
rationale: ROC AUC is rank-based, so calibrating a single model does not help much directly, but blending multiple specialists can fail if their score scales are misaligned. Scikit-learn’s calibration guidance emphasizes disjoint fitting for the calibrator, which fits an OOF specialist-blend workflow.
implementation_hint: Build 2-4 diverse specialists such as the baseline classifier, caution-regime expert, constrained booster, and ID-delayed CatBoost. Generate OOF predictions with race-group folds, calibrate each specialist on held-out folds via sigmoid or isotonic, then compare plain average, rank average, and calibrated logit stacking.
expected_effect: Usually a small but robust LB gain from safer ensembling and less fold-specific score-scale instability.
risk: Extra OOF plumbing; isotonic can overfit if calibration slices are too small.
